rationale.R1.2: A general 'what do you think?' over a research excerpt naturally loads onto logical consistency — specifically whether the inferential chain from data to conclusion is valid. Here the stated conclusions ('substantially outperforms', 'stronger correlation', 'better-calibrated') run directly opposite to the reported numbers (panel rho=0.58 < GPT-4 rho=0.71; panel ECE=0.19 > GPT-4 ECE=0.07), making this dimension central.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: every headline claim in the discussion paragraph is falsified by the numbers in Table 2 as described. The paper asserts the panel achieves higher Spearman correlation and lower ECE than the GPT-4 baseline; the reported figures show the opposite on both metrics. Evidence-claim alignment failure here is not marginal — it invalidates the core empirical contribution.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on factual and logical correctness — specifically whether the conclusions match the reported numbers — or on writing and presentation quality?
